imo the reason they probably see it as a problem is most people spend for a specific character they like. And with 600 pulls you likely get OE and their WoD from just that. Which for a lot of people is enough and sure some will spend to get +12 but a lot of newer people that just like that one character probably won’t. From the losses they can make a decent team and not feel any need to spend for a while. Sure they’ll likely spend on another character in the long run but that’s in my opinion why they see it as a problem. Since it will result in newer players spending a lot less on the game than they would have originally to get OE for that character they enjoy.

This might a bit late to reach you <@1487626955791663208>  light sama but 

I largely consider the state of pandia and alva to be the recession markers of balance.

It's kinda like how with gintama, the second madao gets a job, the series is considered coming close to ending. 

The day that these two characters perform in a way that is similar to the levels of other limited characters, that means the average power of the cast is close to each other.

That is because alva's kit is inherent close to the base gameplay of morimens, while pandia reflects the most basic level of extra mechanics. (Strength stacking/passive damage) 

The futher these two are from the rest of the cast, the clearer you are able to see the game's general power gap. 

I still have faith in you and I am looking forward to these devlogs that will detail plans going forward. 

This is the only way for us to understand what you are trying to do and you can have the time to either alleviate our concerns or take our feedback before you invest development resources into a system.


While you say that it is not your game but ours.

It isn't. It is both. 

Your vision backed by our passion. That is our relationship as gamers to developers. 

We do not wish to hijack or take the helm of your visions. But like every form of work, us players are many so we have many views that we can give to you so that you can choose a path forward.

I would rather feel like we are together in the trenches trying to keep morimens going, than it turning into a tug of war. 

Please keep these sentiments in mind for us. It is a matter of we. Not you or I.

其實怎麼說呢，我對校貓的感情很複雜，一方面不想罵他是基於和他互動時的好感和一些過往的美好回憶，但另一方面又覺得這傢伙沒在進步甚至退步，恨鐵不成鋼的想罵他……他過往真的做過一些贏了我尊重和信任的事，也真的對他很有好感，相信着這個經常搞砸卻依然能帶着🧠團隊和玩家走得遠的傢伙能做出一番成就。

但是他這次真的沒辦法讓我保持對他的尊重和敬愛，他說他做了欠缺考慮的事情，但單單在我眼裡就已經代表着他光說會聽卻沒有真正聽取過社群的意見，社區裡滿是真正用錢支持他們、真正切身體驗遊戲、真正為遊戲做各種(包括同人創作和攻略)貢獻、真正熱愛遊戲的人。他的做法會讓人覺得他只想要取悅貨幣匯率較高的地區的人，既然是出來認錯、擋炮火的，道歉和回覆就該認真且有誠意，而不是用ai生成一個中文回覆，他明明會中文的。可以用ai潤色，但你不能讓人發現你用了ai，不然只會引起更多反對的聲浪，因為在這個世代用ai本身就代表敷衍。

但冷靜地提意見被無視，帶着一些悲傷或者憤怒地想要質詢的情緒的發言又會被製作人視為「不冷靜的發言」……是的，他遭受了很多謾罵，但一開始的謾罵裡也依舊夾雜着建議、或者他們認為製作人哪裡做得不好的觀點。

我是很認真的因為light的一系列操作而失去信任，我很失望。

Be polite可以这样：

分享一个生活小妙招：如何分辨狼和狗？
答：看神态，严肃是狼，笑貌是狗。

这样就很冷静地教会了大家一个新知识<:cute1:1356053053568192717>

怕太長貓不會看，我盡量縮短一點 

1.
既然要推最終法則了，角色體驗關卡設個選擇啟靈數，體驗開個可配備隊伍和為角色設置啟靈數，讓我們有一個直觀的方法知道你賣的+12值不值。

2.
體驗那邊或者喚醒體界面，可不可以有遊戲內的配置+配隊推薦和點讚推薦的功能？或者給角色點讚？不設角色留言了，感覺會增加管理成本。給角色點讚至少可以讓你們對角色廚數量有概念，方便出週邊。

3.
最終法則方面，比起數值加強，也許我們會更在意別的東西。都是角色廚/強度廚才會抽滿吧，那給個+12限定角色頭像/頭像框、個人界面背景裝飾、或者額外的繁衍狂熱劇情/角色故事，就這些可能五選二之類的推出來都能接受，只有通訊錄不夠吸引角色廚。

4.
這麼大的改動，不僅是要提前說，甚至應該在實裝之前先拿出來說。讓我們討論，這樣你又可以發現更多應改未改或者應有未有的東西。讓未來的計劃公開透明，數據留痕，下次(如果有)企劃或者角色設計抄襲事件，不需要你出手，自然就會有玩家維護你和我們的遊戲。

5.
想要典藏館/喚醒體界面放戰鬥小人讓我們可以放大看細節，也想要放大看看一些怪物設計，想要可以查看怪物和他們進攻動畫的功能，因為一些怪物真的很酷。

6.
我愛你和我們的遊戲，校貓，但是你真的讓人愛得很累，下次再有這樣的事我真的不會罵，我會用實質的行動證明我已經完全對你失去信任。即使你用ai敷衍我也沒關係，我最後再信你一次。但真的是最後一次，不要逼我離開我愛的角色和遊戲。

# 忘卻前夜老玩家已成為遊戲發展的阻礙

就應該趕走這些玩了一、二年還反過來黑忘卻的老玩家。
今年真的離大譜，往年是水軍帶節奏，今年是那些老登們帶節奏，真的噁心煩人，要是對手水軍派來擾亂社區也就罷了，可一個個的玩了大幾百天還被人家帶偏，只能說這些老登們是最難伺候的。
最開始帶產能節奏，然後是皮膚的節奏，現在又在瘋狂地帶角色強度甚至最終法則的節奏，自家遊戲自己不護著，一個個的什麼玩意呀，一個個的自私自利，一點都不顧及零氪玩家和萌新的感受。如果能這些老登趕出忘卻前夜，社區環境會好的多。
前瞻还好吧，除了37没狂想其他没有什么大问题吧，累充也是二游正常操作

“what I put in matches what I get back” 
What you've said before made me believe that you're genuinely trying to balance this ideal with still making whaling appealing so that you can profit, which I get. So OE is lower investment but you can keep going after that up to a high dupe rate. Now with adding the axioms the progression doesn't make any sense. You have a huge gap between OE and Axiom and Axiom is 99% for whales which gives them a huge power spike after a huge investment. It sounds good for them but not for any other part of progression. It needs to be more even, your original intent of having amount of allowed dupes so high doesn't fit in this new axion system. The total dupes progression needs to be squashed. And also it's NOT a good idea to stick with OP axioms just because you already announced them. You need to balanced them down before release.

"makes more pay to win, sets a band precedent, think about the future" - are all hypotheticals.

B.I.A.V. as far as we know is not restrained by any investor demands. The only thing they have is their own motivation and the playerbases wishes.

If they like every other gacha were held down by investor and moneymaking expectations. None of these or any of the other choices in the past would have been reverted.

Some OE's already make the game boring to use. P2W is not determined by a unit being overpowered, but main content being unclearable without investment.

I feel like it's genuenly, brain dead to make hypotheticals about a company that owes anyone, outside the players nothing. In their attempt to grow.

If someone wants to AA an op unit and to clear the game easily they should be able to. It's just one more of the countless options in a game with many countless options already.

Granted again people may complain about no content for their op unit. But it was their choice to get to that point. (Plus the Devs can just add the ability to disable enlightens).

Obviously another discussion that comes in mind is P2W Vs f2p content.

Then the Devs decided to add the limited cores. But without telling anywhere outside the patch notes us, added a way to craft selectors. Again, encouraging interaction with the endgame game mode, allowing you to neglect some part of the grind.

I'm genuenly getting tired of all the bitching from the playerbase. Since BIAV owes nothing to no-one. Any change that would ACTUALLY IMPACT HOW YOU PLAY THE GAME FOR THE WORSE. Could be rolled back.

Everyone knows that they would have still played the game with these changes. The greed rope still has a lot of give. It's just instant hate and crying over preconceived notions in the gacha genre again. (1/2)

So are we going to wait till the game almost EOS's again. Overworked Devs get sick and more people quick cause of constant overwork. Or will anyone actually bother to remember why they actually like this game and it's design, and think about if these changes even impact their experiences outside of made up hypotheticals.

I know better then most when it comes to this, cause I had crashed out during the 2nd aniv story balancing. And inevitably, I can comfortably say that at the time I was wrong about the direction of the game...

I still feel the fights in ch9 lost all impact or any clever account wide teambuilding, and none of the arc2 bosses stand up to the gameplay design experience that clearing ch9 was, but for the rest of it. seeing friends start the game, I'm satisfied.

And that's the sort of outlook I have in general about these updates.

They thought about it for a reason. And they thought about it long. An initial freak out based on a bunch of made up info, with no experience or knowledge behind the scenes, means nothing in comparison.

And the Devs should put their foot down at some point. If the situation is bad enough. This game is already way too generous anyways, and if that's unsurvivable, or it makes it impossible to get even close to the quality of arc1 content... I'd rather have the game equal the greed of the rest of the industry.

That's very brave and nice of you, but most gacha games are able to do this because of the sheer weigth of their massive playerbases. 
Morimens has also not been growing which proves people who stick their head in aren't sticking around.

And now new updates are alienating a noticable portion of existing players on top of just trying to monetize them harder through gameplay mechanics, which historically breeds resentment.

You can tout the idea "be as greedy as other gachas", but that only works if players stick around to spend as much as is asked of them.

The existing players have to complain because the alternative is quitting a game they love and letting it die, and the devs have to change their overall strategy based on player wants because the game is not growing and their main source of advertisement is word of mouth.
If your statement is true. What should be done by the developers. Because it's just a loop of stagnation then.

I don't understand how players wanting better communication about huge changes like AA is considered bitching and moaning. 

Especially when Light said before that those AAs would not affect a character's strength in a significant way. And yet, the most broken units get equally broken AAs while others are left to dust. 

We've already seen the hp inflation in dtide with the release of GMurphy. Or the release of more broken units after Tawil and Ghelot, when the devs said they wouldn't do again. 

The bitching and moaning is because people still care about the game. I would be more afraid of its future if they released such changes and everyone would just accept them.
YES! That's what the fuck I've been saying to *the* guy🤦🏻‍♂️

In my opinion, the best way for them to get money would be merch. Like, that's what almost everyone has been asking for. Even some friends of mine, who never have spended on the game, wanted to buy some merch, but it was impossible.

There's several games that live off merch sales, Azur Lane being one example. They don't even need to make gameplay changes, they simply release merch, and people that like the games and it's characters buy it.

I also wanted to try and buy, but it's impossible if you're from Europe. So it's not that people don't want to spend money, but that they can't.

Let's assume for a second they deserve to be as greedy as the industry standard.
Were just out of a full rerun patch. Full rerun patches are, in industry standard, dead patches that bring a tenth of the revenue a unit patch would bring.

Concerning merchandise, there are ways to buy them through proxy services. I won't name them, no point advertising.
But I can't even find the morimens shop through the proxy services.

There's any number of things, but generalised you 1. ensure existing players are satisfied.

Off the top of my head, buff/fix existing characters to be usable without hiding it behind a monetisation scheme.

2. Ensure the new-player experience leaves a good impression. For example 10 different menus of notifications you don't understand or care about and constant pop ups asking you to spend leaves a bad first impression. 

The devs would probably have means to more concretely check where players end up quitting and infer what did it, so the actual changes would depend.

What we have instead is a messy first-impression of menus and game modes with updates focused on 'almost' giving existing players things they have asked for, for ages and a monkeys paw style, but. Like this most recent update technically buffing characters, _but_ you need max duplicates.

This game is also not far from a capital G, gacha despite the generosity of redeems, but it would be healthy to take ideas and inspiration from other games outside the gacha sphere and other games that have had consistent growth inside the gacha sphere.

These games have vastly different scales and financial situations.This title is facing obvious financial pressure right now, unlike big companies with stable revenue.

Unfair power favoritism only accelerates player loss instead of helping the game survive.

Well because theres no comments like this in other gachas its a fact. Other gachas having comments about powercreep of new characters, not powercreep of top-tier whale investment  balance of constellations. Here we have people sh*tting on devs becuase they don't like power level of whale investment tier, which doesn't make any sense. Why exactly characters that whales spent on, should have same power levels as your ftp character? Why exactly old charatcers and especially standard chracters should have the same level of power? So people don't have any incentive at all to spend? Just using standard characters that they have E12 becuase Morimens showering you with pulls? Well, I'm sorry but it's kinda weird?

Morimens is much more of a strategy game than most of the others games, most games that are of similar category are usually fairly balanced on everything that gives you a reward for overcoming challenges, even if you're using older/lower-tier units to play those challenges.

The +12 problem is not only about comparing new limited units to old ones, the game has been escalating difficulty because the new units that are coming out are already WAY stronger compared to old ones and the balancing has been pushing towards their favor, and now you're increasing that disparity by giving them even stronger whale buffs, while giving almost nothing/fixing weaker units only at whale level so they can barely, if even, clear content that was reachable with strategy, which was promised to not be the case before the release.
So they are making "must pull characters," even more desirable, but only at the highest invest, while the other units drown and, if you want to compare it so much to other games, is mainly the case where you start making things harder and harder, older units don't work the same way they used to, which pushes people to NEED more investment.

Even if weaker/older, some characters literally got kit-fixed only at AA. How do you sell a character that needs 15 copies to be fun/decent to use? Answer: If you want your game to live long, you don't. 

The excuse of "but it hasn't happened yet," not only seems to be wrong cause it has already started, but it's the tendency to balance around the new power ceiling, like EVERY other game does with challenge content. 


Some people used way harsher or just hated for free during this thread, but complaining about something that is clearly going in a bad direction cause, if even the people who should be benefiting from this are against it, it's clearly going in a bad direction and criticizing the choices and direction the game is heading is not a bad thing, you get angry when something you like gets bad too.

People are angry because this is an update people have wanted for a very long time, but it is now being added effectively behind a pay wall. On top of a whole bunch of other issues nicely listed in a lot of the chinese comments.

The game IS already escalating without AA even coming out even if you say "it's just your imagination." As was pointed out many times before here, the hp inflation of Tide has been almost in the 300%. But alright, let's say the game IS NOT going to be balanced around AA, let's give them the benefit of the doubt on this one, even though greed is usually a spiral downwards. 

Now, you want o make the comparison of Stand units to Newer units like you did with Genshin?

Well, let's look at the latest units, then.

I don't have Doresain, I pulled for different priorities at the time, and I got no idea how he works, BUT
People have been complaining about him for ages now, his kit seems flawed from the start, I saw people saying he's heavily support dependent(not that big of a flaw cause many dps' are, but if you're locked behind high invest/only high tiers support, you're releasing a mid/bad new dps), like he's missing something in that area too, and +12 FIXES HIS KIT, doesn't even buff it, and he's not even 10 months old iirc.

Fixing characters at the highest level on investment it NOT the move.

What motivates people to spend? Liking the game and mainly THE CHARACTER, QoL for the gameplay, not mandatory copies to make a kit functional(and I'm not even talking about +12 at this point), making content EASIER not CLEARABLE, unless is a mode explicitly said to be FOR WHALES.

I’m actually one of the long-term players who can access E12 content. 

Discussing unfair treatment among high-tier upgrades isn’t wrong at all.Just because only a small percentage of players reach this endgame doesn’t mean biased unit balance should be ignored.This isn’t unrealistic speculation. 

Uneven power gaps at maximum investment directly shape the whole game’s meta and future updates.

All I want is basic respect.

Their stats already fall far behind newer characters, their gameplay isn’t improved at all, and some units are stuck with harsh restrictive conditions from E12. The gap between them is simply ridiculous.

I don't understand your approach to this. Just because it hasn't happened, it doesn't mean that the game isn't headed to that direction. Multiple people have explained why they think that the game is headed towards AA balancing (continuous powercreep) and you keep ignoring those messages.
Also, at one point you need to realize that comparing Morimens to every other gacha is not a reasonable way to look at things. Just because it's worse somewhere else, doesn't mean that people can't complain about what they don't like now

Using your own luck as an argument is so bad tho, "my personal experience says is the same thing cause I got luckier in this game."

Besides, most people who are talking here never attacked the devs as argument, they are doing the same thing as you just did, use personal experience or in some cases even data to form opinions on it, and it seems obviously logical, even if you keep calling it imagination.

Just cause you come from the games where stand/old limited units are COMPLETELY obsolete, it does not mean they have to be here too.

The argument was never about them having the same power level, but the TENDENCY of the other games you love to use as comparison escalating the game so much, not only cause of new units, but of said units having insane copy value that they gotta increase the ceiling, and even if not talking about stand units, older limited units turning almost obsolete even at highest invest too fast is horrible for the game balance and health.

Powercreep is fine, completely normal specially in a gacha game, but giving the already strong units a bigger gap and forcing niche/strategic comps out of field just cause you want to sell more of the units that are already at an advantage, again, IS NOT the move, at least not for the majority of players who are probably low spenders/f2p.

And again, this is not imagination, games tend to do that, the difficulty scales with time and sudden spikes of difficulty because of bad balancing is a pretty normal thing to happen and, just cause is normal, doesn't make it GOOD or FAIR.

**EDIT:** I am extremely hopeful for them to make good decisions even if what I'm saying right now turns to be wrong, but with past mistakes and empty promises(I've playing for almost an year so I spent a chunk of my day yesterday looking stuff up since I lost a bit), which must not be taken as norm but are relevant to the current situation and outrage, you have to be a little skeptical of everything that was said.

For long-time players, with plenty of past pulls and recent fragment adjustments, reaching E12 will become much easier over time.Clearing content has never been hard. All I want is simple fairness and respect for ordinary units.
As for tensions toward devs, it’s more of a cultural expression difference.
Casual discussions in Chinese sound far sharper in English, but they aren’t real malicious attacks. 
I don’t know how to put it better. We speak the same way even to friends and acquaintances. It’s mostly teasing, not genuine attacks on the developers.Of course there are complaints too, after waiting such a long time for these updates.

Future stages may easily become far more difficult. As an old player who experienced 2.0 launch, I know how extreme the power gaps were back then.
You also know how slow this game updates. Buffs for old units used to take half a year to a whole year to arrive, and the improvements were often unsatisfactory.That’s why I’m speaking up now. E12 upgrades are permanent, not temporary version-limited buffs.

Like trying to argue with a broken record. 

The games used as comparison have very similar to completely different issues cause the meta and selling point works differently.

HSR as an example, already selling kits broken into pieces for basic qol that should have been there from the beginning, to the point some Nova Flares(unit buffs) are moving eidolons to base kits and buffing eidolons.

Now let's look at those big games of today, Wuwa, HSR, Genshin, they are based on a SMALL POOL OF ROTATIONAL CHARACTERS/ARCHETYPES, which means that not only the content gets harder cause the new unit is stronger, but also focus solely in that new unit to sell it so you can clear the content easily and move on with your life. You have some outliners that lived a long time in the meta but those are exceptions not the norm.

Morimens game modes rotate between stuff that can help or incentive you to use old units, WHICH IS GREAT, they don't need to be game-breaking like some of the new units, but if the new balancing is already favoring the new units, is not illogical to think it'll push to favoring fully invested units and if you CAN'T see that, not saying that as an insult but you have a very narrow view of things as a whole or are just very gullible.

It's fine try and defend the things you like, personally I find the devs insane(in a good way) because of how they deal with the community, are fairly generous and listen(at least a lot) to the players, even if some people get a bit too entitled because of that.

BUT criticism is good, if done correctly, like many people did. Most people didn't like the changes, and how this IS leading to a bad direction even if you want to deny it and make other excuses, blind trust is a poison not a cure.

If they are going to triple the ceiling of difficulty but not the flooring, a lot of people WILL be dissatisfied with it cause it pushes a "you must whale to clear it" agenda.

Current stages feel fairly manageable, yet I remain deeply concerned about what lies ahead. I’ve witnessed far too many unexpected shifts throughout this game’s lifespan.
Let me restate my point simply: all I ask is basic fairness and dignity for weaker units.
Their base stats already lag far behind newer releases, and I never once wish for old characters to outperform them.I would be more than content with smoother gameplay and looser restrictive mechanics for old units in E12.
